option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
""" Main Demo - calling repository methods to add and retrieve products. """
from srp_product_repository import SrpProductRepository
def main(): """ Main method demonstrates adding products and retrieving the list. """ # Create a product repository repository = SrpProductRepository()
# Add products by providing details (name and price) repository.add_product("Pizza", 500.00) repository.add_product("Burger", 300.00) repository.add_product("Pasta", 400.00) repository.add_product("Salad", 250.00) repository.add_product("Coffee", 100.00)
# Display all products repository.display_products()
# Get the products list and work with it products_list = repository.get_products() print(f"\nTotal products: {repository.get_product_count()}")
# Calculate total value total_value = sum(product.get_price() for product in products_list) print(f"Total Inventory Value: ${total_value}")
# Filter and display expensive products expensive = [p for p in products_list if p.get_price() > 300] print(f"\nProducts over $300: {len(expensive)}") for product in expensive: print(f" - {product.get_name()}: ${product.get_price()}")
if __name__ == "__main__": main()

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
""" Example: Creating a list of SrpProduct objects in Python. """
from srp_product import SrpProduct
def main(): """ Demonstrate creating and managing a list of products. """ # Create a list of products products = [ SrpProduct("Pizza", 500.00), SrpProduct("Burger", 300.00), SrpProduct("Pasta", 400.00), SrpProduct("Salad", 250.00), SrpProduct("Coffee", 100.00), ]
# Display all products print("=== Product List ===") for i, product in enumerate(products, 1): print(f"{i}. {product.get_name()} - ${product.get_price()}")
# Calculate total inventory value total_value = sum(product.get_price() for product in products) print(f"\nTotal Inventory Value: ${total_value}")
# Filter products by price expensive_products = [p for p in products if p.get_price() > 300] print(f"\nProducts over $300: {len(expensive_products)}") for product in expensive_products: print(f" - {product.get_name()}: ${product.get_price()}")
if __name__ == "__main__": main()

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
""" SRP Demo - orchestrator that demonstrates strict Single Responsibility Principle. This is the main entry point showing how each class has only one reason to change. """
from srp_product import SrpProduct from srp_discount_engine import SrpDiscountEngine from srp_shopping_cart import SrpShoppingCart from srp_cart_item import SrpCartItem from srp_inventory_adjustment import SrpInventoryAdjustment from srp_cart_pricing_service import SrpCartPricingService from srp_data_manager import SrpDataManager
def main(): """ Main orchestrator - coordinates different services. Note: This class is an orchestrator, so it's not a strict SRP class, but the individual services it uses follow SRP strictly. """ # Create products product1 = SrpProduct("Pizza", 500.00) product2 = SrpProduct("Burger", 300.00)
# Calculate discounts discount_engine = SrpDiscountEngine() discount1 = discount_engine.calculate_discount_amount(product1) discount2 = discount_engine.calculate_discount_amount(product2)
# Build shopping cart cart = SrpShoppingCart() cart.add_item(SrpCartItem(product1, discount1)) cart.add_item(SrpCartItem(product2, discount2))
# Update inventory inventory = SrpInventoryAdjustment() inventory.update_available_qty(product1) inventory.update_available_qty(product2)
# Calculate total price pricing_service = SrpCartPricingService() total_price = pricing_service.calculate_total(cart.get_items()) print(f"Total price is {total_price}")
# Save to database data_manager = SrpDataManager() data_manager.save_db([product1, product2])
if __name__ == "__main__": main()

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
""" Discount calculation - single responsibility: calculate discount amounts. """
from srp_product import SrpProduct
class SrpDiscountEngine: def calculate_discount_amount(self, product: SrpProduct) -> float: """ Calculate discount for a product without mutating it. """ discount = product.get_price() * 0.1 print(f"Discount for {product.get_name()} is {discount}") return discount

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
updatedContent
